title: Ocugen Q2 2022 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Ocugen Q2 2022 Earnings Call Summary. Key Business Developments: Covaxin Vaccine Program: Ocugen is advancing its COVID-19 vaccine partnership with Bharat Biotech. The company is progressing the Phase 2/3 immuno-bridging trial (OCU002) and planning to initiate an adult safety clinical trial pending FDA approval. Management noted that Covaxin provides durability through immune memory and a broader immune response compared to existing mRNA vaccines. Gene Therapy Initiatives: The OCU400 program for Retinitis Pigmentosa completed Cohort 1 dosing, with the independent safety board recommending advancement to Cohort 2, expected to begin in August 2022. The company anticipates efficacy data around six to twelve months post-dosing. OCU410 for dry age-related macular degeneration is in pre-IND studies, with Phase 1/2 initiation targeted for 2023. Cell Therapy Expansion: Ocugen announced NeoCart, a tissue-engineered cartilage therapy for knee cartilage repair. The company received RMAT designation from the FDA. Financial Position: Cash on hand totaled $115 million as of June 30, 2022, with management estimating runway into Q2 2023. The net loss for Q2 was approximately $19.5 million.
